resolutions.content: 3.3.1 Text Size - GAP WCAG - 1.4.4 & 1.4.8 limits size to 200%, NOGAP UAAG met by 1.4.1 & 1.4.2
3.3.2 Font - GAP WCAG no SC, NOGAP UAAG met by 1.4.1 & 1.4.2
3.3.3 Style - GAP WCAG no SC, NOGAP UAAG met by 1.4.1 & 1.4.2
3.3.4 Capitalization - GAP WCAG no SC, NOGAP UAAG met by 1.4.6

3.4.6 Spacing - GAP WCAG 1.4.8 only covers

, NOGAP UAAG met by 1.4.2 and 1.4.3

3.4.6 Spacing - GAP WCAG 1.4.8 only covers

, NOGAP UAAG met by 1.4.2 and 1.4.3
No SC in WCAG, UAAG covers in 14.2 and 1.4.6 ... No SC in WCAG, UAAG covers in 14.2 and 1.4.6 ... No SC in WCAG, UAAG covers in 14.2
GAP in WCAG, Covered in 1.8.6 and 1.8.9
GAP in WCAG, UAAG GAP with partial 1.10.1
GAP in WCAG, UAAG 2.71 covers UA items only, not content elements
WCAG partial 1.44 and 1.48, UAAG 2.71 covers UA items only, not content elements
GAP in WCAG, Covered in UAAG 1.4.4
3.7.3 user agents uses OS settings; content uses UA and OS settings
Absent in WCAG; NOGAP UAAG GL 2.6 and 5.1.3

resolutions.content: Gap in LVTF 3.3.5 - UAAG and WCAG only cover text
change 3.4.1 change leading to be "line spacing (leading)" eliminate line height
GAP - WCAG - partial, no adjusting, only minimum), UAAG (adjustable - only to 2x, with 3 levels). Note: users may want less than minimuim
GAP not covered by WCAG or UAAG
GAP - WCAG 1.4.8 partial - no full justifiaction, NO GAP UAAG 1.4.3 blocks of text
GAP not covered by WCAG, no gap in UAAG (though border settings not fully expanded)
